incidents.vector: Exploited known vulnerabilities in 18,000+ end-of-life SOHO routers (TP-Link, MikroTik) to hijack DNS settings and intercept OAuth tokens via adversary-in-the-middle attacks.
incidents.impact: Over 200 organizations and 5,000 consumer devices compromised across 120+ countries; authentication tokens for Microsoft Office accounts stolen from government agencies, military, and critical infrastructure. DOJ conducted court-authorized Operation Masquerade to remediate US routers.

incidents.victim: US water, energy, and municipal critical infrastructure
incidents.vector: Exploited internet-facing operational technology (OT) devices including Rockwell Automation PLCs via CVE-2021-22681, manipulating SCADA/HMI displays.
incidents.impact: Operational disruptions and financial losses at multiple US water and energy utilities. At least 3,900 devices in crosshairs. Attacks escalated in response to US-Iran military conflict. Minot, ND water treatment plant hit separately by ransomware.

incidents.victim: Axios npm package users (100M+ weekly downloads)
incidents.vector: Social-engineered the Axios maintainer to compromise npm and GitHub accounts, injected a hidden RAT dependency (plain-crypto-js) into versions 1.14.1 and 0.30.4.
incidents.impact: Cross-platform RAT deployed on Windows, macOS, and Linux systems worldwide. OpenAI forced to rotate macOS signing certificates. Thousands of downstream projects potentially compromised during the 3-hour window malicious versions were live.

incidents.victim: LiteLLM, Trivy, Checkmarx, European Commission, Mercor
incidents.vector: TeamPCP group used stolen credentials to inject credential-harvesting malware into trusted open-source tools (LiteLLM, Trivy, Checkmarx GitHub Actions), cascading across five software ecosystems in five days.
incidents.impact: Massive credential theft across cloud environments (AWS 36%, Azure 61%). European Commission AWS account breached with 90GB+ data exfiltrated by ShinyHunters using stolen credentials. Mercor ($10B AI startup) also confirmed compromised. Group still active and claims ongoing operations.

incidents.vector: Hackers posed as a professional trading firm at a crypto conference, deposited $1M to build trust, then compromised developer devices via malicious Apple TestFlight app and VS Code extensions to obtain multisig transaction approvals.
incidents.impact: $280 million in cryptocurrency stolen. Attack was prepared weeks in advance with extensive social engineering. Linked to same group behind $1.5B Bybit heist.

incidents.victim: Healthcare, education, and finance organizations (US, UK, Australia)
incidents.vector: Medusa ransomware group (Storm-1175) exploited zero-day vulnerabilities in SmarterMail and GoAnywhere MFT, moving from initial access to data exfiltration and encryption within 24 hours.
incidents.impact: University of Mississippi Medical Center and Passaic County NJ among confirmed victims. Hospital required FBI and DHS assistance to reopen. Microsoft warns group can weaponize vulnerabilities before public disclosure.

incidents.victim: Cloud workloads on AWS, GCP, Azure, and Alibaba Cloud
incidents.vector: Deployed an undetectable ELF backdoor on Linux cloud instances using SMTP port 25 as covert C2 channel, with typosquatted domains mimicking Alibaba Cloud services to obscure traffic.
incidents.impact: Cloud credentials harvested from AWS, GCP, Azure, and Alibaba environments. Backdoor carries zero VirusTotal detections and represents six years of investment in cloud-native tooling. Stolen credentials enable privilege escalation and lateral movement across cloud services.

incidents.vector: Exploited zero-day vulnerability CVE-2026-34621 (CVSS 8.6) in Adobe Acrobat/Reader via maliciously crafted PDF files requiring only that the victim open the file.
incidents.impact: Active exploitation since at least November 2025. Malware fingerprints victims' systems, exfiltrates local files, and can deploy follow-on RCE and sandbox escape exploits for full system control. Adobe patched April 11.

incidents.vector: Exploited zero-day improper access control vulnerability CVE-2026-35616 (CVSS 9.8) in FortiClient EMS endpoint management tool, enabling remote code execution.
incidents.impact: Nearly 2,000 publicly exposed instances identified. Active exploitation began March 31 and ramped up after hotfix release. CISA added to KEV catalog. Full patch still pending — only hotfix available.

incidents.vector: Exploited CVE-2026-3502 in TrueConf video conferencing software's update mechanism to distribute malicious payloads to dozens of government agencies via compromised on-premises server.
incidents.impact: Dozens of government entities across one Southeast Asian country received weaponized updates through the trusted TrueConf update channel. ShadowPad malware deployed. CISA ordered federal agencies to patch within two weeks.

incidents.vector: Exploited CVE-2026-32201 zero-day in SharePoint Server to spoof trusted content and interfaces, enabling phishing and social engineering within SharePoint environments.
incidents.impact: Actively exploited in the wild. Part of a massive 167-vulnerability Patch Tuesday release. BlueHammer privilege escalation bug (CVE-2026-33825) in Windows Defender also patched after exploit code published. Six .NET CVEs patched including two RCE flaws.

incidents.vector: ShinyHunters group accessed Rockstar's Snowflake account via a breach of third-party analytics platform Anodot, exfiltrating 78 million records.
incidents.impact: Stolen data includes GTA Online and Red Dead Online revenue metrics, player behavior tracking data, and game economy data. Snowflake preemptively disabled all Anodot-linked accounts.

incidents.vector: Ransomware attack that forced shutdown of affected systems; company deployed intermediary infrastructure to continue operations.
incidents.impact: Order processing and shipping disrupted. In SEC filing, Hasbro said recovery would take weeks. No ransomware group has publicly claimed the attack.

incidents.vector: Pro-Russian hacker group with links to Russian intelligence attempted to breach operational technology (OT) systems at a thermal power plant in western Sweden.
incidents.impact: Attack was unsuccessful due to built-in security protections. Sweden's defense minister disclosed the incident, noting similar attempts across Norway, Denmark, and Poland. Represents a tactical shift from DDoS to destructive OT attacks.

incidents.vector: Security breach of Booking.com systems exposed customer personal data.
incidents.impact: Booking details, names, email addresses, home addresses, and phone numbers exposed. No financial data compromised. PIN numbers reset for affected reservations.

incidents.vector: ShinyHunters group breached Hallmark's Salesforce accounts and leaked data after ransom was refused.
incidents.impact: Personal information of 1.7 million customers leaked publicly after Hallmark refused to pay ransom demand.

incidents.vector: Successful phishing attack led to breach of the major US corporate law firm's systems.
incidents.impact: Customer materials posted online by hackers. Jones Day employs more than 2,400 lawyers and is known for representing high-profile clients including former President Trump.

incidents.victim: Systems using Iran timezone or Farsi locale
incidents.vector: TeamPCP group deployed CanisterWorm wiper malware via exposed Docker APIs, Kubernetes clusters, and Redis servers, targeting systems matching Iran's timezone or Farsi default language.
incidents.impact: Data destruction on systems matching Iranian locale. Represents an unusual escalation from a financially motivated group into geopolitically targeted wiping operations amid the US-Iran conflict.
